cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. So the user is asking about a World Bank study from 1974 and what it found regarding economic growth in some backward countries. First, I need to recall the passage provided. The key part here is the paragraph that mentions the 1974 World Bank study. The passage says, "A major study by the World Bank in 1974 concluded that it is now clear that more than decades of rapid growth in developing countries has been of little benefit to a third of their population." Wait, let me parse that again. The wording is a bit tricky. The study concluded that rapid growth had been of little benefit to a third of their population. So that means that a third of the population didn't benefit much, implying that the rest (the other two-thirds) might have benefited. But the question is phrased as "brought _."
